title: Streaming Export Foundation
impact: 50-70% memory reduction for large exports by streaming data to files instead of building in memory

tasks.description: Create StreamingExporter Utility - Create src/features/StreamingExporter.ts with methods for streaming JSONL and CSV exports

imports: import type { Entity, Relation, KnowledgeGraph } from '../types/types.js';

stepByStep: Create new file: src/features/StreamingExporter.ts
Add the imports at the top of the file
Export the StreamResult interface with all 4 fields
Create the StreamingExporter class with private filePath property
Implement constructor that takes filePath and stores it
Implement streamJSONL: create write stream, iterate entities writing JSON.stringify + newline, iterate relations, wait for stream end
Implement streamCSV: write header row, iterate entities calling entityToCSVRow, wait for stream end
Implement entityToCSVRow: escape quotes by doubling, join fields with commas
Add JSDoc documentation for class and public methods
Run: npm run typecheck
acceptanceCriteria: streamJSONL writes entities and relations as JSONL
streamCSV writes header and entity rows with proper escaping

tasks.description: Add Streaming Export Constants - Add STREAMING_CONFIG constant to src/utils/constants.ts
stepByStep: Open src/utils/constants.ts
Find the end of the existing constants (after SIMILARITY_WEIGHTS or similar)
Add a JSDoc comment: /** Streaming export configuration */
Add: export const STREAMING_CONFIG = { ... } as const;
Add all 4 fields: STREAMING_THRESHOLD (5000), CHUNK_SIZE (500), HIGH_WATER_MARK (64 * 1024), FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS (100)
Run: npm run typecheck

tasks.description: Create Streaming Export Unit Tests - Create tests/unit/features/StreamingExporter.test.ts with tests for JSONL and CSV streaming

imports: import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest';
helpers: createTestGraph(entityCount: number): KnowledgeGraph - Creates N test entities with observations and tags
testSuites: streamJSONL - 3 tests for small, large, empty graphs
streamCSV - 2 tests for header and special char escaping
Memory efficiency - 1 test for large graph memory check

stepByStep: Create new file: tests/unit/features/StreamingExporter.test.ts
Add all imports at the top
Create helper function createTestGraph(entityCount) that returns KnowledgeGraph with N entities
Create describe('StreamingExporter') block
Add beforeEach to create temp directory using tmpdir() and Date.now() for uniqueness
Add afterEach to clean up temp directory with fs.rm(testDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
Add describe('streamJSONL') with 3 tests: small graph (10 entities), large graph (1000 entities), empty graph
Add describe('streamCSV') with 2 tests: graph with header verification, special characters escaping
Add describe('Memory efficiency') with 1 test checking memory increase is < 10MB for 5000 entities
Run: npx vitest run tests/unit/features/StreamingExporter.test.ts
acceptanceCriteria: 8+ tests covering JSONL, CSV, and memory efficiency

tasks.description: Update Barrel Export - Export StreamingExporter from src/features/index.ts
stepByStep: Open src/features/index.ts
Add: export { StreamingExporter, type StreamResult } from './StreamingExporter.js';
Run: npm run typecheck
Run: npm test

successCriteria: StreamingExporter class created with JSONL and CSV methods
notes: This sprint creates the foundation for streaming exports. The StreamingExporter is a standalone utility that can stream graph data to files without loading everything into memory. This enables handling graphs larger than available RAM.
